description_long: This is a compact folding karambit knife with a pronounced curved blade and a finger ring at the end of the handle. The blade is single-edged, black, and features a hawkbill shape, ideal for slicing and hooking motions. The handle is skeletonized with multiple circular cutouts, likely for weight reduction and grip enhancement, and has a camouflage pattern. A visible pocket clip is attached to one side, allowing for convenient carry. The knife appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with some possible polymer or composite elements in the handle. The overall design is tactical and utilitarian, suitable for urban utility or self-defense scenarios.
description: A compact folding karambit knife with a curved black blade, camouflage-patterned skeletonized handle, and a finger ring. Features a pocket clip and multiple grip-enhancing cutouts.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved blade, camouflage handle, finger ring, and pocket clip.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the knife's slim profile and the alignment of the blade and handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade curve, handle cutouts, and finger ring from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, showing the thinness of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: skeletonized, contoured, camouflage-patterned, finger ring, pocket clip
